content.full_text:
THOMAS L. PADBERG
Post Office Box 4338
San Clemente, CA 92674
714-498-5844

MEMORANDUM

Date: December 13, 1990

To: M. Crispino; T. Dunn; R. Heil; L Henricks; P. Linden; S. Diehl; M. Parness

From: T. Padberg [signature]

RE: Protest of Closed Session Scheduled to be Conducted as Part of Special
Meeting of Golf Course Committee of the City of San Clemente on December
13, 1990 at 5:00 PM.

*************************************************************************

Please consider this memorandum to be my official protest of the subject closed session
because the subject closed session violates the Brown Act of the State of California.

The subject closed session was announced as follows on 12-12-90: "Closed session to
discuss property negotiations pursuant to Government Code Section 54956.8. Meeting
with Mayor Diehl and the Golf Course Committee regarding property negotiations as they
relate to the proposed Foothill Corridor. Property located in Camp Pendleton - State
Parks Leasehold. Negotiator - (USMC)."

The Code Section pursuant to which this closed session is being called reads as follows:
"Notwithstanding any other provision of this Chapter, a legislative body of a local agency
may hold a closed session with its negotiator prior to the purchase, sale, exchange or lease
of real property, by or for the local agency, to give instructions to its negotiator
regarding the price, terms of payment for the purchase, sale, exchange or lease.
However, prior to the closed session, the legislative body of the local agency shall hold
an open and public session at which it identifies the real property or real properties to
be negotiated which the negotiations may concern, and the person or persons with whom
the negotiator may negotiate. For purposes of this section, the negotiator may be a
member of the legislative body at the local agency; for purposes of this section, lease
includes renewal or renegotiation of the lease. Nothing in this section shall preclude the
local agency from holding a closed session for discussions regarding eminent domain
proceedings pursuant to Section 54956.9." (emphasis added.)

The proposed closed session is illegal for the following reasons:
1) the required prior public session has not been held;
2) the referenced Code Section empowers legislative bodies ONLY to hold closed
session for the purpose(s) stated therein. The Golf Course Committee is NOT a
legislative body; refer to Section 1, Resolution 84-81 of the City Council of the
City of San Clemente, which defines the powers and duties of the Golf Course
Committee (copy attached).

cc: all City Council members;
Col. E. J. Turse, USMC - Camp Pendleton;
A. Oliver, Dist. Supt., CA Dept. of Recreation and Parks;
Fair Political Practices Commission
Attorney General, State of California

NOTICE AND CALL OF SPECIAL MEETING
OF THE GOLF COMMITTEE

TO MEMBERS OF THE SAN CLEMENTE GOLF COURSE COMMITTEE

NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that a special meeting of the San Clemente
Golf Course Committee is hereby called to be held on Thursday, December 13, 1990, at 5:00
PM, in the Mayor's Conference Room, City Hall, 100 Ave Presidio, San Clemente, CA.
92672.

Said special meeting shall be held to consider the following agenda item(s).
(If not stated here or on agenda, it cannot be discussed.)

1. Closed session to discuss property negotiations pursuant to Government Code Section
54956.8. Meeting with Mayor Diehl and the Golf Course Committee regarding property
negotiations as they relate to the proposed Foothill Corridor. Property located in Camp
Pendleton - State Parks leasehold. Negotiator- (USMC)

DATED: 12-12-90

[illegible]
CHAIRPERSON

RESOLUTION NO. 84-81

RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF SAN
CLEMENTE, CALIFORNIA, ESTABLISHING A GOLF COURSE COMMITTEE

WHEREAS, the Golf Course Committee was first established by minute
order of the City Council on April 7, 1976; and

WHEREAS, various amendments have been made thereto; and

WHEREAS, the Golf Course Committee feels that a more specific set
of instructions should be compiled;

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the City Council of the City of
San Clemente as follows:

SECTION 1. There is hereby established a Golf Course Committee
consisting of five members which shall have the power and duty to:

(A) Act in an advisory capacity to the City Council in the
planning pertaining to the use and operation of the
Municipal Golf Course.

(B) Make suggestions and recommendations to the City Manager and
City Council pertaining to the Annual Budget for the Golf
Course during the process of its preparation.

(C) Evaluate and review the restaurant lease; golf pro agreement;
and facilities development plans of the golf course.

(D) Assist in the preparation of rate schedules for the users of
the golf course and make recommendations regarding same to
the City Council.

(E) Perform such other duties as may be prescribed by the
City Council.

SECTION 2. The terms of members of the Committee shall be for two
years and said terms shall be staggered so that two members shall be appointed
one year and three members shall be appointed the following year. The concluded
terms of office shall expire on June 30th of each year. For the original
appointments the City Council shall appoint three members for two year terms
and two members for one year terms.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a closed session of the Golf Course Committee regarding property negotiations violates the Brown Act because no prior public session was held and the Golf Course Committee is not a legislative body authorized to conduct such closed sessions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/protest memorandum to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No FPPC conclusion or advice section is present in the document. The document includes the protest memo, notice of special meeting, and relevant resolution establishing the Golf Course Committee's powers. Addressed to multiple recipients including the FPPC, City Council, and other agencies.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a closed session of the Golf Course Committee regarding property negotiations violates the Brown Act because no prior public session was held and the Golf Course Committee is not a legislative body authorized to conduct such closed sessions.

Supt., CA Dept. of Recreation and Parks; Fair Political Practices Commission Attorney General, State of California NOTICE AND CALL OF SPECIAL MEETING OF THE GOLF COMMITTEE TO MEMBERS OF THE SAN CLEMENTE GOLF COURSE COMMITTEE NOTICE IS HEREBY GIVEN that a special meeting of the San Clemente Golf Course Committee is hereby called to be held on Thursday, December 13, 1990, at 5:00 PM, in the Mayor's Conference Room, City Hall, 100 Ave Presidio, San Clemente, CA. 92672.
embedding.summary: This is a memorandum protesting a closed session scheduled by the Golf Course Committee of San Clemente on December 13, 1990, arguing that the closed session violates the Brown Act because the required prior public session was not held and the Golf Course Committee lacks statutory authority to conduct closed sessions for property negotiations.
